questions.question: Regarding data ordering natively within Apache Kafka, what is the strict architectural guarantee?
options: Kafka guarantees perfect chronological order across the entire global Topic regardless of infrastructure.
Kafka mathematically guarantees sequential ordering strictly only within a single isolated Partition, but makes absolutely no guarantees regarding order across multiple different partitions.
Kafka permanently scrambles all message orders to maximize network bandwidth.
Kafka sorts all strings alphabetically before writing them to disk.
questions.answer: Kafka mathematically guarantees sequential ordering strictly only within a single isolated Partition, but makes absolutely no guarantees regarding order across multiple different partitions.
questions.explanation: If a transaction must be sequential, you must ensure both events share the exact same 'Partition Key' so they are routed to the same partition sequentially.

questions.question: What is the primary architectural purpose of assigning multiple individual applications to the exact same Kafka 'Consumer Group'?
options: To explicitly delete old log messages safely.
To strictly ensure that every single application mathematically receives an exact duplicate copy of all network messages.
To allow Kafka to intelligently divide the Topic's Partitions among the applications, gracefully sharing the massive streaming workload safely and securely without any data duplication.
To forcefully translate JSON outputs into GraphQL.
questions.answer: To allow Kafka to intelligently divide the Topic's Partitions among the applications, gracefully sharing the massive streaming workload safely and securely without any data duplication.
questions.explanation: If 5 workers share the same group ID, Kafka acts as a fair load-balancer, uniquely partitioning the data uniquely among them.

questions.question: What happens if a Kafka Topic currently has exactly 4 Partitions, but your active Consumer Group explicitly contains 6 identical backend applications?
options: Kafka instantly safely creates 2 new partitions dynamically.
Kafka flawlessly assigns 1 partition to each of the first 4 applications, and the remaining 2 applications will structurally remain entirely idle, doing completely nothing.
Kafka encrypts the data flawlessly across all nodes.
questions.answer: Kafka flawlessly assigns 1 partition to each of the first 4 applications, and the remaining 2 applications will structurally remain entirely idle, doing completely nothing.
questions.explanation: A single partition can never be read by two workers in the same group, otherwise data would be duplicated.
